lunch.name: Grilled chicken breast with quinoa and roasted vegetables

instruction: Generate a structured gym program for a 30-year-old male, goal: muscle gain, intermediate level, 4 days per week, access to full gym

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Control the descent, 2 second pause at bottom

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Break parallel, knees track over toes

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Full range, do not lock knees

progression.rule: Add 2.5kg when you complete all sets at the top of the rep range for 2 consecutive sessions

instruction: Generate a structured gym program for a 35-year-old female, goal: fat loss, beginner level, 3 days per week, home gym with dumbbells only

exercises.name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
exercises.notes: Hold one dumbbell at chest, squat to parallel

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at hips, feel hamstring stretch

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Press overhead, do not arch lower back

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge
exercises.notes: Step back, keep front knee over ankle

exercises.name: Dumbbell Chest Fly on Floor
exercises.notes: Wide arc, slight bend in elbows

exercises.name: Burpees
exercises.notes: Modify by stepping instead of jumping if needed

progression.rule: Increase reps by 2 each week until top of range, then increase weight by 2kg

instruction: Generate a structured gym program for a 50-year-old male, goal: maintenance and mobility, intermediate level, 3 days per week, full gym, knee injury history

exercises.name: Cable Chest Press
exercises.notes: Cable preferred over barbell - less joint stress

exercises.name: Leg Press (partial range)
exercises.notes: Do not go below 90 degrees, protect knees

exercises.name: Foam Rolling - Quads and IT Band

output.injury_notes: Avoid deep squats and lunges. All leg exercises performed in pain-free range only. If knee pain exceeds 3/10, stop and rest.

progression.rule: Add 1 rep per set each week. Increase weight only every 3 weeks.

instruction: Generate a grocery list based on the following weekly meal plan for fat loss, male, 2000 kcal/day

output.storage_notes: Store salmon and turkey mince in freezer. Buy fresh chicken and use within 2 days or freeze.

instruction: Generate a grocery list for a vegetarian female, goal: muscle gain, 2400 kcal/day, weekly shop for one person
Generate a complete health plan including meal plan, gym program and grocery list for a 38-year-old male, 82kg, goal: performance optimization for recreational cyclist

meal_plan.strategy: Periodised nutrition - higher carbs on training days, lower on rest days

breakfast.name: Overnight oats with banana, nut butter and honey

during_ride.name: Energy gels or dates
during_ride.notes: Every 45 minutes for rides over 90 minutes

post_ride.name: Chocolate milk or protein shake with fruit

lunch.name: Pasta with tomato sauce, chicken and vegetables

dinner.name: Steak with roasted potatoes and salad

gym_program.name: Cycling Strength Support - 2x per week
gym_program.focus: Prevent muscular imbalances, support power output

sessions.focus: Upper Body and Hip Flexor Mobility

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.notes: Critical for cyclists - hip flexors tighten

key_items.notes: Primary carb source on training days

instruction: Generate a meal plan for a 55-year-old female, 70kg, post-menopausal, goal: maintenance with focus on bone health and cardiovascular health

nutritional_priorities: Omega-3 fatty acids for cardiovascular protection
Adequate protein to prevent sarcopenia: 1.2g per kg bodyweight minimum
Phytoestrogens from soy and flaxseed for hormonal balance

breakfast.name: Fortified soy milk porridge with ground flaxseed and strawberries
breakfast.notes: Soy milk provides phytoestrogens and calcium. Flaxseed provides lignans and omega-3.

mid_morning.name: Low fat cheese with whole grain crackers

lunch.name: Sardine salad on whole grain bread with salad leaves
lunch.notes: Sardines with bones are exceptionally high in calcium and omega-3

afternoon_snack.name: Greek yogurt with almonds and dried apricots

dinner.name: Baked mackerel with roasted Mediterranean vegetables and quinoa
dinner.notes: Mackerel is high in omega-3 and vitamin D

supplement_recommendations: Calcium citrate: 500mg twice daily with meals if dietary intake insufficient
Magnesium glycinate: 300mg before bed - supports bone and sleep

instruction: Generate a gym program for a 22-year-old male, goal: muscle gain, advanced level, 5 days per week, full gym, wants to compete in bodybuilding

exercises.name: Incline Barbell Press
exercises.notes: Primary compound for upper chest development

exercises.name: Tibialis Raise
exercises.notes: Shin muscle for aesthetics and balance

Friday.focus: Pull - Bicep and Rear Delt Dominant

progression.rule: Complete all sets at top of rep range for 2 sessions, then add 2.5kg next session

instruction: Generate a complete health assessment and plan for an obese 40-year-old female, 110kg, 165cm, goal: fat loss, has hypertension, cleared by GP for exercise

key_priorities: Low sodium: under 1500mg per day due to hypertension
High fibre: 30g+ per day for satiety and cardiovascular health
Avoid processed and packaged foods (high hidden sodium)
Adequate protein to preserve muscle during weight loss: 100-120g per day

breakfast.name: Poached eggs on whole grain toast with sliced tomatoes

lunch.name: Homemade vegetable and lentil soup with seed bread

dinner.name: Grilled salmon with steamed broccoli and cauliflower mash

gym_program.name: Low Impact Fat Loss - Beginner
gym_program.notes: All exercises low impact to protect joints at current weight. Progress gradually.

week_5_to_8.notes: Increase duration of cardio to 25 minutes. Add one more resistance exercise.

week_9_to_12.notes: Aim for 30 minutes cardio. Begin pool walking if available - excellent low impact option

output.medical_notes: Monitor blood pressure before and after exercise. Stop if dizzy, chest pain or BP exceeds 170/100. Report any symptoms to GP. Lisinopril may cause dizziness on rapid position changes - rise slowly.

instruction: Generate a high protein meal plan for a 26-year-old male, 75kg, goal: muscle gain, 3000 kcal/day, lactose intolerant

meal_plan.notes: All dairy replaced with lactose-free or plant alternatives. High protein target: 165g per day.

breakfast.name: 5-egg omelette with oats and avocado on the side

mid_morning.name: Protein shake with banana and almond butter

lunch.name: Ground beef rice bowl with black beans and vegetables

dinner.name: Baked chicken thighs with sweet potato and asparagus

evening.name: Lactose-free coconut yogurt with mixed nuts

grocery_highlights: Oat milk (not almond - higher calories for muscle gain)
Plant protein powder (pea + rice blend for complete amino profile)
Lactase enzyme tablets if occasional dairy unavoidable

instruction: Generate a maintenance meal plan for a healthy 33-year-old female athlete, 58kg, 165cm, trains 6x per week mixed CrossFit and running

meal_plan.notes: High training volume requires adequate carbohydrate fuelling and protein for recovery. Do not restrict calories.

breakfast.name: Smoothie bowl: banana, berries, spinach, protein powder, topped with granola and seeds

mid_morning.name: Whole grain bagel with smoked salmon and cream cheese

pre_workout.name: Banana and energy chews or dates

lunch.name: Chicken rice salad with avocado and tahini dressing

afternoon_snack.name: Greek yogurt with honey and walnuts

dinner.name: Beef stir fry with noodles and vegetables

meal_plan.rest_day_adjustment: Reduce carbohydrates by 100g, increase fats slightly. Target 2200 kcal on rest days.

instruction: Generate a gym program for a 29-year-old female, goal: fat loss and toning, beginner, 4 days per week, full gym access, wants to lose 10kg

output.weight_loss_notes: At 4 sessions per week plus daily walking, expect 0.5-0.75kg fat loss per week. Scale weight may fluctuate due to water retention - focus on measurements and strength progress, not just the scale.

instruction: Generate a 7-day meal plan for a 31-year-old male, 78kg, goal: muscle gain, 2800 kcal/day, gluten intolerant

meal_plan.notes: All grains replaced with naturally gluten-free alternatives: rice, oats (certified GF), quinoa, potatoes.

breakfast.name: Certified GF oats with protein powder, banana and nut butter

lunch.name: Rice bowl with teriyaki salmon, edamame and pickled ginger

dinner.name: Grilled steak with roasted sweet potato and green salad

snack.name: Rice cakes with cottage cheese and tomato

breakfast.name: Eggs Benedict on GF English muffin with smoked salmon

lunch.name: Quinoa tabbouleh with grilled chicken and hummus

dinner.name: Thai green curry with chicken and jasmine rice (GF curry paste)

snack.name: Greek yogurt with GF granola and berries

important_notes: Always check labels - soy sauce contains gluten, use tamari
Oats must be certified gluten free
Curry pastes and spice mixes may contain gluten - check packaging
Beer is not gluten free - opt for cider or GF beer

instruction: Generate a grocery list for a low carb diabetic male, 45 years old, fat loss goal, weekly shop for one person

vegetables_low_carb.notes: Moderate carb - limit to 1 per day

items_to_avoid: Low fat products (often high in sugar)

instruction: Generate a complete plan for a 19-year-old male student, 70kg, skinny, goal: muscle gain, 3200 kcal/day, limited budget, can cook basic meals

meal_plan.philosophy: Budget bulking - maximise protein and calories per euro spent. Simple recipes only.

breakfast.name: 4 scrambled eggs with 100g oats and whole milk

lunch.name: Tinned tuna pasta with olive oil and cherry tomatoes
lunch.instructions: Boil 100g pasta. Drain. Mix with 2 tins tuna, olive oil, tomatoes, salt, pepper. Done.

snack.name: Peanut butter and banana sandwich on whole grain bread

dinner.name: Chicken thighs (bone-in, cheap) with rice and frozen vegetables
dinner.instructions: Season chicken with salt, pepper, garlic powder. Bake at 200°C for 35 min. Boil 100g rice. Microwave 150g frozen veg.

evening.name: Cottage cheese with peanut butter and honey

output.gym_program_note: Focus on compound lifts: squat, deadlift, bench, row, overhead press. 3x per week full body. Progressive overload is everything. Eat more than you think you need.

instruction: Interpret blood test results and provide health constraints for a 42-year-old male planning a fitness program

flags.clinical_note: Elevated LDL increases cardiovascular risk. Dietary intervention recommended.
Prediabetic range. Monitor carbohydrate quality and quantity.

dietary_constraints: Include omega-3 rich foods 3x per week
exercise_constraints: Cleared for moderate to vigorous exercise
Aim for 150 minutes moderate cardio per week
interpretation.follow_up: Retest lipid panel and HbA1c in 3 months

instruction: Interpret blood test results and provide health constraints for a 25-year-old female athlete planning a muscle gain program

flags.clinical_note: Low ferritin indicates depleted iron stores. Common in female athletes. Can impair performance and recovery.
Mildly anaemic. Consistent with low ferritin.

dietary_constraints: Prioritise iron-rich foods: red meat 2x per week, lentils, spinach
Pair iron sources with vitamin C to enhance absorption
Avoid tea and coffee within 1 hour of iron-rich meals
exercise_constraints: Avoid maximal effort training until ferritin improves above 20 ug/L
supplement_recommendations: Iron supplement: consult GP for dose
interpretation.follow_up: Retest full blood count and ferritin in 6-8 weeks
